name: Cameroon

outbound_official.note: no official Cameroon government outbound visa-list page located; rely on destination-side data. The Cameroon Ministry of External Relations (MINREX, diplocam.cm) and related official domains (passcam.cm, dgsn.cm, evisacam.cm, prc.cm) do not publish an outbound list of visa-free/visa-on-arrival/e-visa destinations for Cameroon ordinary-passport holders. All official Cameroon government consular pages focus exclusively on inbound visa services for foreigners visiting Cameroon. The MINREX Twitter handle (@diplocam_minrex) and Facebook (minrex.cm) were also checked — no such list was published there either. No analog to India's mea.gov.in/vffin page was found for Cameroon.

visa_free.notes: CEMAC member. Ordinary passport holders can enter Cameroon visa-free under the CEMAC Free Movement Agreement. Multiple official and aggregator sources (Embassy of Cameroon Japan, Wikipedia, cameroon-visa.info) confirm 90-day visa-free access for all CEMAC ordinary passport holders. Previous record grouped this under a CEMAC bloc entry only; now listed individually per CEMAC membership.

CEMAC member. Ordinary passport holders can enter Cameroon visa-free under the CEMAC Free Movement Agreement. Previously MISSING from this record despite being a founding CEMAC member. Multiple sources confirm 90-day visa-free access (Wikipedia Visa policy of Cameroon; cameroon-visa.info nationals-entering-cameroon-visa-free). ADDED in this validation pass.
CEMAC member. Nationals explicitly exempt from visa requirement. Confirmed on Official Embassy of Cameroon in Egypt visa exemption page. Also covered by CEMAC Free Movement Agreement.
CEMAC member. Nationals explicitly exempt from visa requirement. Confirmed on Official Embassy of Cameroon in Egypt visa exemption page. Also covered by CEMAC Free Movement Agreement.
CEMAC member. Nationals explicitly exempt from visa requirement. Confirmed on Official Embassy of Cameroon in Egypt visa exemption page. Also covered by CEMAC Free Movement Agreement.

Free movement for all nationals — need only present a valid official document. Bilateral agreement. Confirmed on Official Embassy of Cameroon in Japan visa page (cameroonemb-jp.org) and Official Embassy of Cameroon in Egypt visa exemption page. Multiple aggregators confirm 90-day stay.

Ordinary passport holders can enter Cameroon visa-free under the Agreement on the Free Movement of Persons and Goods. Previous record described this as an 'expense-free (gratis) visa' but multiple sources (Wikipedia, cameroon-visa.info, Embassy Japan) consistently categorise this as visa-free entry. CORRECTED classification from gratis-visa to visa_free in this validation pass.

e_visa.nationality: All other nationalities (ordinary passport holders not covered by exemption agreements)
e_visa.notes: All ordinary passport holders not covered by bilateral exemption agreements must apply online through the official evisacam.cm portal (operated by MINREX). Short-stay visa up to 6 months (180 days) — normal processing 72 hours, fee EUR 153; express processing 24 hours, fee EUR 229. Long-stay visa up to 12 months (360 days) — fee EUR 305. Transit visa up to 4 days (gratis) or more than 4 days (paid). Visa sticker affixed either at embassy/high commission or at border entry point. Launched April 30, 2023 per Decree No. 2023/147 of 02 March 2023 implementing Law No. 97/012 of 10 January 1997. Fees confirmed by Cameroon High Commission UK FAQ page.

visa_policy.notes: Cameroon operates a universal e-visa policy via evisacam.cm (MINREX) effective April 30, 2023 under Decree No. 2023/147 of 02 March 2023. All ordinary passport holders must apply online unless covered by a bilateral exemption agreement. Seven countries qualify for visa-free ordinary passport entry: all 5 other CEMAC members (Equatorial Guinea, Gabon, Republic of Congo, Chad, Central African Republic) plus Nigeria and Mali. Diplomatic and Service/Official passport holders have additional bilateral visa-waiver agreements in force with: Equatorial Guinea, Egypt, Mali, Brazil, Vietnam, Turkey, China, UAE, Switzerland, Italy, Russia, Tunisia, Algeria. Agreement with Republic of Korea noted as signed June 2024 but not yet ratified. Yellow fever vaccination certificate required for all entrants.

conditional_access.conditions: Equatorial Guinea — bilateral visa exemption agreement in force (CEMAC member); exact date not published on checked official sources. Ordinary passport holders separately covered by CEMAC free movement.

Egypt — bilateral visa exemption agreement in force for diplomatic and service passport holders. Ratification date reported as December 29, 2023 by non-official sources; official decree text on prc.cm not directly verified.
Bilateral visa exemption agreement for diplomatic and service passport holders in force; ordinary passport holders separately covered by Free Movement of Persons and Goods agreement (visa-free).
Bilateral visa exemption agreement ratified by Cameroon on April 25, 2025 per non-official sources; official decree text not directly verified.
Bilateral visa exemption agreement signed December 21, 2024 in Algiers; ratified by President Biya on January 29, 2026 per non-official sources. Official decree text on prc.cm not directly verified.
Bilateral visa exemption agreement in force; exact date not published on checked official sources.
Bilateral visa exemption agreement in force for diplomatic passports only (not service passports); 30 days. Exact agreement date not published on checked official sources.
Bilateral visa exemption agreement in force; 90 days within any 6-month period. Exact agreement date not published on checked official sources.
Bilateral visa exemption agreement in force; 30 days. Exact agreement date not published on checked official sources.
Bilateral visa exemption agreement in force for diplomatic passports only (not service passports); 30 days. Exact agreement date not published on checked official sources.
Bilateral visa exemption agreement in force; exact duration and date not published on checked official sources.
Bilateral visa exemption agreement in force; 90 days within any 6-month period. Exact agreement date not published on checked official sources.
Bilateral visa exemption agreement in force; 90 days within any 180-day period. Exact agreement date not published on checked official sources.

cbi.notes: No official Citizenship-by-Investment program found for Cameroon. No official government source publishes any such program. Aggregators confirm the absence of a CBI program.

programs.name: Long-Stay Visa (Aliens — investors/workers)
programs.notes: Cameroon issues long-stay visas (up to 12 months) under Law No. 97/012 of 10 January 1997 and Decree No. 2023/147 of 02 March 2023 for aliens wishing to reside or work in Cameroon. Categories include: contract workers, independent workers, long-term trainees, students, and family members of aliens staying in Cameroon. Employment contracts must be signed by the Minister in charge of employment. No specific investor minimum investment amount is published on official sources. No formal 'golden visa' or investor-specific residence program with defined thresholds exists on official government websites.

programs.name: Short-Stay Express Visa (evisacam.cm)
programs.eligibility: All ordinary passport holders eligible to apply for a Cameroon visa (same eligibility as standard short-stay visa)
programs.notes: Express processing option available through the official evisacam.cm portal (MINREX) at an additional fee. Standard short-stay visa costs EUR 153 (approx. XAF 100,000); express short-stay costs EUR 229 (approx. XAF 150,000). Valid for up to 6 months (180 days). Standard processing is 72 hours (3 working days). Long-stay visa EUR 305. Fees confirmed by Cameroon High Commission UK FAQ page. Referenced in Decree No. 2023/147 of 02 March 2023.

agreements: CEMAC free movement (Communauté Économique et Monétaire de l'Afrique Centrale) — 6 member states: Cameroon, Equatorial Guinea, Gabon, Republic of Congo, Chad, Central African Republic

validation_notes: ADDED Gabon (GAB) to visa_free: Gabon is a CEMAC member and was erroneously absent from the prior record's visa_free list despite CEMAC membership being noted in the group entry. Confirmed by Wikipedia Visa policy of Cameroon and cameroon-visa.info.

ADDED Equatorial Guinea (GNQ) to visa_free as a separate individual entry: Prior record had it only in a grouped 'CEMAC nationals' entry. All 7 visa-free countries are now listed individually for clarity and graph-node correctness.

CORRECTED Mali (MLI) classification: Prior record described Mali's ordinary passport access as 'expense-free (gratis) visa' in the notes, implying a visa is required but waived in cost. Multiple official and aggregator sources (Embassy Japan, Wikipedia, cameroon-visa.info) consistently categorise this as visa-free entry under the Agreement on the Free Movement of Persons and Goods. Reclassified to visa_free.

REMOVED the grouped CEMAC bloc entry: Prior record had a single entry for 'CEMAC nationals (Equatorial Guinea, Gabon, Republic of Congo, Chad, Central African Republic) — biometric passport holders' with iso3: null. Replaced with individually listed entries per country so the directed-graph model can resolve each nationality correctly.

ADDED max_stay_days: 90 to all visa-free entries: Previously all visa-free entries had max_stay_days: null. Multiple independent sources (Wikipedia, cameroon-visa.info) confirm 90-day stays for visa-free visitors.

CORRECTED the conditional_access entry for Equatorial Guinea: Previously had eligible_nationalities: 'any' with ambiguous phrasing. Corrected to eligible_nationalities: ['GNQ'] since this entry covers Equatorial Guinea's diplomatic/service passport exemption specifically.

UPDATED e-visa fee amounts in fast_track and e_visa notes: Added EUR amounts (EUR 153 short-stay standard, EUR 229 short-stay express, EUR 305 long-stay) as confirmed by Cameroon High Commission UK FAQ page, in addition to XAF amounts.
UPDATED agreements field: Added full list of 6 CEMAC member states for clarity.
ADDED ambacam.de to official_domains: Cameroon Embassy Berlin website checked during this validation pass.
gaps: Specific list of ALL visa-exempt ordinary passport nationalities not published verbatim on a single official Cameroon government page (minrex.gov.cm or prc.cm); individual bilateral agreements are referenced but no consolidated official list found.

Mali free movement agreement for ordinary passports confirmed from Embassy Japan page but full agreement text not found on official sources; max_stay_days of 90 sourced from aggregators, not a primary official government page.
Nigeria free movement for all nationals mentioned in official Embassy Japan and Embassy Egypt pages but no treaty text found on official .gov.cm source.

Gabon ordinary passport visa-free status confirmed from aggregator sources (Wikipedia, cameroon-visa.info) only — no direct official Cameroon government source (.gov.cm) explicitly lists Gabon as visa-free; inferred from CEMAC membership and consistent aggregator consensus.

Equatorial Guinea ordinary passport visa-free status: same gap as Gabon — no single official .gov.cm page explicitly lists GNQ as visa-free for ordinary passports; confirmed via CEMAC and aggregator sources.
Residence permit minimum investment thresholds for investors not published on official government sources.
Dual citizenship policy not explicitly stated on official government pages found.
Path to permanent residency or citizenship timelines not published on official pages found.
Republic of Korea agreement signed June 2024 but not yet ratified — current status uncertain.
MINREX official website (minrex.gov.cm) returned DNS resolution error and could not be accessed during this validation pass.
No official English-language consolidated visa exemption list found on a single .gov.cm domain.

No third-country credential conditional access rules found on any official Cameroon government source: no provision for holders of US visa, Schengen visa, UK visa, Canadian visa, Australian visa, residence permits, or OCI cards to enter Cameroon visa-free or on arrival.

Exact agreement dates for bilateral diplomatic/service passport waivers with Brazil, Switzerland, China, Italy, Russia, Turkey, UAE, Vietnam, Equatorial Guinea, and Mali not found on official government sources.
Egypt, Tunisia, and Algeria decree dates sourced from Cameroon Intelligence Report (not a .gov domain); official decree text on prc.cm not verified for each country.
UAE and Vietnam waiver scope (diplomatic only vs. diplomatic+service) confirmed from aggregators, not a primary official government source.
